Cape Town International Airport Wins Dual World Airport Awards

At the 2025 Skytrax World Airport Awards, Cape Town International Airport won two prestigious honors: "Africa's Best Airport" and "Best Airport Staff Service in Africa". These "Oscars of the Airport Industry" not only recognize service excellence but also signal new opportunities for South Africa property investment.

Africa's Best Airport
Best Airport Service
Skytrax Rated
LP
Leo Pan

Founder, DingYao Advisory

  1. Home
  3. Blog
  5. Cape Town Airport Wins World Airport Awards

At the annual highlight of the global aviation industry—the Skytrax World Airport Awards 2025 ceremony— Cape Town International Airport (CPT) won two prestigious honors: "Africa's Best Airport" and "Best Airport Staff Service in Africa". This is not just a headline for the airport, but an important investment signal: South Africa's infrastructure and international competitiveness are steadily improving, and the golden window for property investment has opened.

#1
Africa's Best Airport
Skytrax 2025

Airports Rated
Worldwide

Survey Participants
2025
Award Year
World Airport Awards

World Airport Awards: The Oscars of the Airport Industry

The World Airport Awards is a global airport rating event organized by the UK-based aviation research company Skytrax, held annually since 1999 and known as the "Oscars of the Airport Industry". Unlike other awards judged by industry experts or media, the World Airport Awards are based on actual passenger experiences and satisfaction surveys from travelers worldwide.

According to official Skytrax data, the 2025 survey covered more than 575 airports worldwide, with participants from over 100 nationalities. The survey spanned several months and evaluated dozens of criteria including airport clearance efficiency, facility comfort, dining options, shopping experiences, and staff service attitude. Winning such a rigorous evaluation fully demonstrates Cape Town Airport's international standards.

"The World Airport Awards represent the most credible evaluation in the global airport industry because they directly reflect the actual experiences of millions of travelers."— Edward Plaisted, CEO of Skytrax

Cape Town Airport's Advantages: Why Africa's Best?

Cape Town International Airport is not only the face of South Africa but also the aviation hub of Southern Africa. According to official ACSA data, the airport handled 11.4 million passengers in 2024, serving over 30 international airlines and connecting to more than 50 destinations worldwide. Behind these numbers lies Cape Town Airport's excellence across multiple dimensions:

1. Modern Facilities and Design

Cape Town Airport's international terminal opened in 2009, designed by renowned architectural firms, blending modern architectural aesthetics with African cultural elements. Large expanses of glass curtain walls allow natural light to fill the entire terminal, and passengers can enjoy spectacular views of Table Mountain while waiting. The terminal features over 50 duty-free shops, 30+ restaurants, and comprehensive business lounge facilities.

2. Exceptional Service Quality

The "Best Airport Staff Service in Africa" award is high recognition of Cape Town Airport staff professionalism. From check-in counter staff and security personnel to lounge service teams, Cape Town Airport employees are known for their friendliness, professionalism, and efficiency. In multilingual services, airport staff can fluently communicate in English, Afrikaans, and basic French, German, and Chinese to meet the needs of global travelers.

3. Efficient Clearance Process

For international travelers, clearance efficiency is an important criterion for evaluating airports. Cape Town Airport excels in this area, with average immigration clearance times controlled within 30 minutes, and smooth outbound security screening processes. The airport is also actively introducing biometric technology and automation equipment to further enhance clearance efficiency.

4. Seamless Transportation Connections

Cape Town Airport is only about 20 kilometers from the city center, with a travel time of approximately 20-30 minutes. The airport offers multiple transportation options, including MyCiTi rapid bus service, taxis, car rentals, and pre-booked private vehicles. This convenient transportation connection allows both business travelers and tourists to easily travel between the airport and the city.

Comparison with Other African Airports: Cape Town's Unique Advantages

The African continent has numerous international airports, including Johannesburg's O.R. Tambo International Airport (JNB), Casablanca's Mohammed V International Airport (CMN), and Cairo International Airport (CAI). Cape Town Airport's ability to stand out among these strong competitors and become "Africa's Best Airport" lies in its unique comprehensive advantages:

Cape Town Airport vs. Other Major African Airports

✓ Safety Advantage

Compared to Johannesburg Airport, Cape Town's surrounding areas have better security conditions, making it more attractive to international travelers.

✓ Natural Scenery

The airport area features world-class natural scenery including Table Mountain and the Cape of Good Hope—unique assets that other African airports cannot match.

✓ International Connectivity

Dense direct flights to Europe, the Middle East, and Asia make it the preferred gateway for business and leisure travelers.

✓ Modern Facilities

The terminal underwent a complete renovation in 2009, with facilities far more modern than many older African airports.

Impact on South Africa Property Investment

For real estate investors, Cape Town Airport winning these awards is not just a headline but an important investment signal. The airport's enhanced international reputation will affect surrounding real estate markets on multiple levels:

1. Increased International Business Travel Demand

World Airport Awards recognition will further enhance Cape Town's international visibility, attracting more business travelers and international conferences to choose Cape Town as their destination. According to Invest Cape Town analysis, international business travelers typically increase by 10-15% within 2-3 years after an airport wins such awards. This will directly drive demand for business apartments and serviced residences around the airport.

2. Corporate Relocation and Employment Growth

Quality airport infrastructure is an important factor for multinational corporations when choosing regional headquarters. Cape Town Airport's international recognition will attract more international companies to establish operations, creating high-paying employment opportunities. These professionals will become a stable tenant base for surrounding high-end residential properties.

3. Tourism Property Appreciation Potential

Cape Town is already a popular global tourist destination, and airport awards will further strengthen this image. For investors in the short-term rental market, this means higher occupancy rates and rental yields. Properties within a 15-30 minute drive from the airport will become preferred accommodation areas for tourists.

"Airports are the face of a city. Quality airport services can significantly enhance a city's international image, thereby driving activity in the real estate market."— Airports Council International (ACI) Research Report

Future Development: Airport Upgrade Plans and Investment Opportunities

The awards are just the beginning—Cape Town Airport's future is even more promising. Airports Company South Africa (ACSA) has announced investments exceeding 10 billion Rand in airport upgrade plans, with the goal of increasing annual passenger capacity from the current 14 million to 20 million by 2030. This upgrade program includes terminal expansion, runway extension, and smart airport construction, which will further enhance Cape Town Airport's competitiveness.

For investors, this means 2026-2030 is the golden entry window. As the airport upgrade plans progress, areas such as Somerset West, Stellenbosch, and Bellville around the airport are expected to see significant property price increases and rental yield growth. Historical data shows that airport upgrade projects of similar scale typically drive surrounding property prices up by 10-25% within 3-5 years.

Why Choose DingYao Advisory?

As the Crestline Advisory exclusive Asia-Pacific digital marketing center, DingYao Advisory focuses on helping high-net-worth Taiwanese individuals invest in South Africa. Faced with the investment opportunities created by Cape Town Airport's awards, our advantages include:

  • 1 Strategic Local Partnership: Deep collaboration with Crestline Advisory, providing first-hand investment opportunities around Cape Town Airport, with carefully selected high-potential properties in Somerset West, Stellenbosch, and other prime areas
  • 2 Digital Asset Platform: Exclusive Client Portal system allows you to monitor overseas asset status in real-time from Taiwan, tracking value growth driven by airport upgrades
  • 3 Capital Security: Investment funds are deposited in Standard Bank personal dedicated accounts, FICA compliant, absolutely secure and fully traceable
  • 4 Professional Market Insights: In-depth analysis of how airport awards and infrastructure upgrades impact real estate markets, developing optimal investment strategies for you

Currently, our carefully selected projects such as the Durbanville Premier Estate, Stellenbosch Historic Residence, Constantia Luxury Green Belt are all within Cape Town Airport's value radiation range. As the airport's international reputation improves and upgrades progress, the investment value of these areas will continue to rise.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the World Airport Awards?
World Airport Awards is a global airport rating event organized by UK aviation research company Skytrax, known as the "Oscars of the Airport Industry". The awards are based on passenger satisfaction surveys covering over 575 airports and more than 100 nationalities worldwide, making it one of the most credible evaluations in international aviation. Rating criteria include airport clearance efficiency, facility comfort, dining options, shopping experience, and staff service attitude across dozens of dimensions.
How does Cape Town Airport winning these awards affect real estate investment?
Airport awards signify improved passenger experience and service quality, attracting more international business travelers and tourists, which drives real estate demand in surrounding areas. Historical data shows that areas around award-winning airports typically see significant growth in rents and property prices within 2-3 years after winning, especially for business apartments and high-end residential projects. Additionally, quality airport infrastructure attracts multinational corporations to establish operations, creating high-paying employment opportunities and further boosting residential rental demand.
What advantages does Cape Town Airport have compared to other African airports?
Cape Town Airport is not only one of Africa's busiest airports but is also renowned for its superior location, modern facilities, and excellent service. Compared to Johannesburg Airport, Cape Town offers more beautiful natural scenery (Table Mountain) and a safer surrounding environment; compared to other major African airports, its international route network is more extensive, connecting major cities in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. Additionally, Cape Town's international reputation as a tourist destination provides the airport with stable business and leisure traffic.
How can investors capitalize on the opportunities created by Cape Town Airport winning these awards?
Investors can partner with DingYao Advisory and our South African partner Crestline Advisory to acquire property in high-potential areas around Cape Town Airport such as Somerset West, Stellenbosch, and Bellville. As the airport's reputation improves and infrastructure develops, these areas are expected to see significant capital appreciation and rental yield growth. We recommend investors seize the entry opportunity during 2026-2028, securing quality properties before the airport upgrade plans are completed.
